entries.title: Families file wrongful death lawsuit in downstate Chatham crash that killed 4 children, teen counselor
entries.summary: <p>The families of four kids and a teenage camp counselor killed in a crash last year in downstate Chatham are suing the driver and the day camp.</p><p>The lawsuit was filed Sunday in Sangamon County, almost a year after a driver crashed into YNOT After School Camp, which holds after-school programs and summer camps.</p><p><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/crime/2025/04/28/4-children-killed-when-driver-crashes-into-after-school-program-downstate" target="_blank" >On April 28, 2025</a>, Marianne Akers, 44, was driving on County Highway 5A when she drove off the road, traveled through a cornfield and crashed into an outer wall on the east side of the building.</p><p>Kathryn Corley, 7, Alma Buhnerkempe, 7, Bradley James Lund, 8, Ainsley Johnson, 8, and 18-year-old counselor Rylee Britton were killed in the crash.</p><p>The lawsuit claims the camp was built in violation of safety codes prohibiting permanent youth camps from being located within 100 feet of a highway. It also claims the building did not have adequate means installed to prevent a vehicle from striking the building.</p><p>The lawsuit also accuses Akers of negligence for failing to stop the vehicle before crashing into the building. Akers was not injured in the crash and no charges were filed.</p><p>“No family should ever have to endure the unimaginable loss of a child. Kathryn, Alma, Bradley, Ainsley and Rylee had their whole lives ahead of them,” Lance D. Northcutt, the attorney representing the families, said in a statement.</p><p>“Chatham is a tight-knit community, and this tragedy has deeply affected countless families. These children were irreplaceable. With this lawsuit, the families seek accountability for this profound loss and meaningful safety improvements to prevent a tragedy like this from ever happening again.”</p><p>Camp officials couldn't be reached for comment.</p><p>At least five other children were hurt in the crash.<br></p>
entries.author: Cindy Hernandez

entries.title: Chicago area air quality among worst in U.S., report finds
entries.summary: <p>More than 2 million kids in Illinois are breathing unhealthy levels of air pollution, according to new report issued on Wednesday by the American Lung Association to mark Earth Day.</p><p>The yearly "State of the Air" report found nearly half of U.S. children are breathing polluted air. According to the report, the Chicago-Naperville metro area was named the 15th most polluted in the nation for ozone pollution and received an F grade. Statewide, the report found just over 2.2 million children are experiencing unhealthy air, the report found. </p><p>Nationwide, the report found 33.5 million children — 46% of people younger than 18 — live in areas that received a failing grade for at least one measure of air pollution. Across all age groups, 44%, or 152 million people, also live areas that received a failing grade for at least one measure of air pollution. </p><p>While there were some improvements in terms of particle pollution, the report found worsening impacts of ozone. </p><p>"Clean air is essential to the health and well-being of families across Illinois. Children deserve to breathe air that won't make them sick," Kristina Hamilton, director of advocacy for the American Lung Association, said in a news release accompanying the release of the report. "Unfortunately, too many people in the Greater Chicago area are living with unhealthy levels of ozone and particle pollution. This air pollution is causing kids to have asthma attacks, contributing to chronic health conditions and making people who work outdoors sick."</p><p>On July 31, <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/weather/2025/07/31/chicago-worst-air-quality" target="_blank" >Chicago had the worst air quality in the world</a> according to IQ Air, a Swiss air-technology company. Canadian wildfire smoke was to blame for the hours a thick smoke blanket coated the metro area. According to the Lung Association's report, the Chicago-Naperville area experienced 18 days of unhealthy air quality in 2025, three fewer days than in 2024.</p><p>Under the Trump administration, rollbacks on healthy air rules by the Environmental Protection Agency aren't making the situation better, according to the report.</p><p>Hamilton said Illinois policymakers must take action to improve air quality. She also urged the Chicago City Council to pass the Hazel M. Johnson Cumulative Impacts Ordinance, <a class="Link" href="https://www.chicago.gov/city/en/depts/mayor/press_room/press_releases/2025/april/Hazel-Johnson-Cumulative-Impacts-Ordinance.html" target="_blank" >introduced by Mayor Brandon Johnson last week</a>. The ordinance includes several measures intended to protect those Chicago neighborhoods bearing an unfair portion of the city's air pollution.</p><p>She also said she urges support for state-level legislation to curb warehouse pollution and expand infrastructure, such as charging stations, that help support zero-emission vehicles.</p>
entries.author: Elleiana Green

entries.title: Wrigley rat spooks Phillies, annoys Cubs brass
entries.summary: <p>The Canada goose that parked itself in the Wrigley Field outfield Monday night, now that was funny.</p><p>The Norway rat that jumped off a bag of balls in the eighth inning Tuesday night, skittered across the shoes of Phillies manager Rob Thomson, sprinted the length of the visitors’ dugout, dashed past the photographers’ well and disappeared behind the Reynolds Wrap tarp rolled up along the wall?</p><p>Funny might be a stretch, particularly for an unnerved Phillies bench, players jumping back in surprise. “Scared a little bit,’’ said Phillies catcher Rafael Marchan, unhappy to be so close to the trespassing rodent. And a camera operator stationed next to the dugout was rattled, saying he hated rats.</p><p><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/cubs" >Cubs</a> management was anything but amused.</p><p>When the Sun-Times called asking to speak with Patrick Meenan, senior vice president of operations, the call was routed to media relations.</p><p>“We take the health and safety of our guests seriously,’’ the Cubs said in a statement that was released to those outlets who made inquiries. “As an open venue in a big city, rodent abatement is something we do year-round. We understand how important it is for our fans to have a positive experience, and keeping our ballpark clean and safe is always a top priority.’’</p><p>The statement was issued “on behalf of the Cubs organization.’’</p><p>But while one official said the club preferred the focus be on the team’s winning streak — the Cubs beat the Phillies 7-2 on Wednesday night for their eighth straight win — the rat was a singular sensation.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" data-embed> <blockquote class="instagram-media" cellphone video posted on X by Marcus Leshock, a WGN-TV feature reporter on the station’s morning show, was viewed nearly two million times, according to the reporter, and retweeted all over cyberspace.</p><p>Thomson took it in stride. No, he said, he’d never seen a rat on the field during a game.</p><p>“He ran right, almost right over my toes,” Thomson said. “I could see him over there, sitting on that ball bucket, and I knew he was gonna keep on coming. Started running, came right by the dugout.</p><p>“I’ve seen squirrels. Last year in [Citizens Bank Park], it was at night, a squirrel ran right over my hand.’’</p><p>Thomson thought a bit more. “I take it back,’’ he said. “I saw rats in Shea.’’</p><p>Cubs fans, like Gail Palmier, still have memories of the black cat that suddenly appeared in front of the Cubs’ dugout in Shea Stadium in September 1969, which some fans still interpret as a harbinger of the team’s infamous collapse against the Mets.</p><p>Gail and her husband have been Cubs season-ticket holders since 2014, with seats 10 rows from home plate. They noticed a commotion along the front row, then saw the rat make its dash.</p><p>In the seven years they’ve occupied those seats, they’d never seen a rat during a game. “But when we’re walking back to our car after the game,’’ Gail said, “we see rats.’’</p><p>That should hardly come as a shock in a town that was voted “rattiest city in America’’ by the Orkin pest control people for 10 consecutive years until Los Angeles claimed the distinction last year.</p><p>And rats in ballparks are hardly a new phenomenon. Jim Deshaies, the Cubs’ TV analyst, used to pitch for the Astros and recalls cats roaming in the bowels of the old Astrodome.</p><p>“Feral cats versus the killer rats,’’ Deshaies said. “Didn’t know which side to hang with.’’</p><p>For the manager occupying the home dugout, it was almost a circle of life moment.</p><p>“The rat’s got a job,’’ the Cubs’ Craig Counsell said. “He’s got a job to do. He’s cleaning up some garbage, you know? The cat’s got a job — eat the rat.’’</p><bsp-listcarousel class="PageListE" data-module data-column-count=3 data-hide-authors="true" data-hide-categories="true" data-hide-dates="true" entries.title: Bears exit from Chicago closer to reality as House passes bill helping Arlington Heights bid
entries.summary: <p>SPRINGFIELD — Illinois lawmakers inched closer to the red zone in keeping the Chicago Bears from crossing state lines into Indiana, with the Illinois House on Wednesday green-lighting a measure that would give the NFL team property tax certainty while also providing some property tax relief for homeowners.</p><p>The House voted 78-32 to approve the megaproject proposal, which still needs approval from the Illinois Senate. It would incentivize the Bears to build a domed stadium in Arlington Heights by negotiating payments in lieu of property taxes, known as PILOT, based on assessments. It also directs those funds toward property tax relief for homeowners — both near the megaproject and throughout Illinois.</p><p>The bill represents the first major legislative step toward ensuring the Bears stay in Illinois in a months-long tug of war between Illinois and the Hoosier state. The thorny details of infrastructure funding for Arlington Heights — which are likely to include help for Chicago — must still be dealt with before lawmakers adjourn from the spring session next month.</p><div class="RelatedList Enhancement" data-module data-align-center> <div class="RelatedList-title">Related</div> <ul class="RelatedList-items"> <li class="RelatedList-items-item"> <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears/2026/04/17/bears-hammond-indiana-stadium-stadium-site-meeting-kevin-warren-george-mccaskey-thomas-mcdermott-nfl-roger-goodell" >Bears bosses and Hammond mayor meet on proposed stadium site</a> </li> </ul> </div> <p>“This bill says Illinois is not going to sit back and watch other states build the future while we argue over yesterday's tools,” said State Rep. Kam Buckner, chief sponsor of the measure. “This bill gives us the chance to compete and not compete recklessly, not compete desperately, not compete by giving away the store.”</p><p>During the House debate, State Rep. Patrick Windhorst, R-Metropolis, asked Buckner directly whether Gov. JB Pritzker’s office is on board with the bill, and if it would ensure the Bears stay in Illinois. </p><p>“I don’t work for the Bears, so I can’t answer that question specifically, but what I can tell you, Leader Windhorst, is that this bill puts together the mechanism that makes it possible for the Bears to stay here in the state,” Buckner said. “I think that for… their own internal conversations, there needs to be more conversation about infrastructure and other things.”</p><p>Pritzker's office called the House vote "an important step," adding, “Governor Pritzker has been clear and consistent for years that the Bears should remain in Illinois, and that any legislation needs to protect taxpayers."</p><p>Buckner this week added a <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/bears-stadium/2026/04/20/bears-stadium-proposed-legislation-arlington-heights-property-tax-relief-illinois-homeowners-northwest-indiana-site" target="_blank" ><u>property tax relief sweetener</u></a> to try to secure some bipartisan support and to eliminate the optics of billionaire owners getting a special deal.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-240000" name="image-240000"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/096f7f0/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5712x3206+0+539/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F44%2Fd5%2F8309cc0240aba9931ceb2bcc3a24%2Fimg-9792.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/faf0702/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5712x3206+0+539/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F44%2Fd5%2F8309cc0240aba9931ceb2bcc3a24%2Fimg-9792.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/4798059/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5712x3206+0+539/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F44%2Fd5%2F8309cc0240aba9931ceb2bcc3a24%2Fimg-9792.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="State Rep. Kam Buckner, D-Chicago, briefed lawmakers on the latest changes to the megaprojects bill during a House Revenue Committee hearing on Wednesday afternoon. " srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/4798059/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5712x3206+0+539/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F44%2Fd5%2F8309cc0240aba9931ceb2bcc3a24%2Fimg-9792.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/5c6bc53/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5712x3206+0+539/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F44%2Fd5%2F8309cc0240aba9931ceb2bcc3a24%2Fimg-9792.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/4798059/2147483647/strip/true/crop/5712x3206+0+539/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F44%2Fd5%2F8309cc0240aba9931ceb2bcc3a24%2Fimg-9792.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>State Rep. Kam Buckner, D-Chicago, briefed lawmakers on the latest changes to the megaprojects bill during a House Revenue Committee hearing on Wednesday afternoon. </p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Tina Sfondeles/Chicago Sun-Times </p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>The latest proposal would put 50% of special PILOT payments into a property tax relief fund that would then be divided 60% toward property tax rebates for homeowners in the district where the megaproject is located, and 40% into the Illinois Property Tax Relief Fund. Buckner, before a House panel Wednesday, called the provision the piece he “is most proud of.”</p><p> “This is not a tax holiday,” Buckner said. “The project still pays property taxes on a frozen basis, and it must also make a negotiated special payment.” </p><p>The project must invest at least $100 million in eligible costs to qualify — and the megaproject developer must enter into an incentive agreement with the local municipality, a project labor agreement with a goal of putting 20% or more of project-related contracts to minority-owned businesses and having the company’s CEO consider the economic benefits the project brings to underserved communities. </p><p>A local review board would be created with members of the local taxing bodies, like school districts and others, to decide the amount of the special payment from the developers on a weighted vote.</p><p>Buckner said language was added to exclude data centers from the legislation after lawmakers expressed concerns.</p><p>Buckner has emphasized that while the Bears have received all the attention on the legislation, the proposal is intended to help the state go head to head with states like Michigan, Ohio and Texas, which have enacted megaproject legislation of their own.</p><p>“This is about making Illinois competitive, but in a way that keeps people in the center and focus of this,” Buckner said. “Just because someone builds a bridge and someone is the first person to cross that bridge, doesn’t mean that bridge is built for them.”</p><p> The proposal would also bar state and local elected officials from receiving discounted tickets to stadium events connected to a megaproject — and bars all state officials, local officials and their representatives from working for any megaproject certificate holder if they personally and substantially participated in negotiation of the megaproject agreement. </p><p>The Bears issued a statement welcoming "the progress made on the House’s version of the mega project bill; however, additional amendments are necessary to make the Arlington Heights site feasible for our stadium project. We support Illinois leaders as they determine the path forward to making the essential changes to the mega project bill and aligning on infrastructure funding.”</p><p>Republicans, for the most part, voted against the measure, saying it would inadvertently raise taxes for property owners in other taxing districts that overlap with Arlington Heights but don’t actually include the site of the stadium project. Nine Republicans, however, broke rank and voted for the bill. Five Democrats voted against it.</p><p>Marty McLaughlin, R-Barrington Hills, during House floor debate, had urged his GOP colleagues to join him in moving the plan forward, saying the longer lawmakers wait, the more expensive it will be to build the stadium.</p><p>“One thing I do know is my friends on the other side of the aisle and the governor certainly cannot afford for the Bears to leave the state of Illinois,” McLaughlin said. “Let's face it, guys, it's going to happen, and the longer we wait, I can't watch billions of dollars more in incentives be thrown away.” </p><p>In a statement, Illinois Senate President Don Harmon, D-Oak Park, offered little on what he thinks of the latest proposal, saying there are “ongoing conversations, and the Senate would certainly review any legislation the House sends over.”</p><p>Local school districts and school groups have long raised concerns on property tax valuations around the stadium site. Illinois Federation of Teachers President Stacy Davis Gates, in a memo obtained by WBEZ, says they are pleased with a change in the proposal that ensures school districts won’t get less money than what they would get in the first year of the project. But Gates, who also heads up the Chicago Teachers Union, said the plan doesn’t do enough to fund local public schools.</p><p>“We don’t believe it sufficiently captures the new property value of these developments," Gates said, "so schools don’t get as much new revenue as we believe they’ll need.”</p>
entries.author: Matt Trunfio

entries.title: Will this time be different? Sky push for fresh start
entries.summary: <p>There are two distinct feelings coming out of <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/chicago-sky" target="_blank" >Sky</a> training camp.</p><p>One comes from Courtney Vandersloot and Azura Stevens. They helped deliver the Sky’s only WNBA title in 2021 and want to get back to that standard.</p><p>The other comes from coach Tyler Marsh and general manager Jeff Pagliocca, who would like a fresh start.</p><p>And who wouldn’t, after the last two years?</p><p>Losing seasons, coaching turnover, and a practice facility that still isn’t ready — despite being promised by the end of 2025.</p><p>Despite all that baggage, the front office managed to put together a competitive roster for the 2026 season.</p><p>There’s the intriguing new frontcourt pairing of Kamilla Cardoso and Rickea Jackson, a rather accomplished backcourt featuring Skylar Diggins and Vandersloot and a lottery pick in Gabriela Jaquez, fresh off an NCAA title at UCLA.</p><p>‘‘I think we should take a moment to step back and see what this is gonna look like,’’ Pagliocca said Wednesday. ‘‘Because it’s an awfully exciting, energetic group that we’ve put together.’’</p><p>You can understand that sentiment, can’t you? That desire to turn a new leaf, to honor the potential?</p><p>You also can understand the hesitation from fans. So many disappointments since the 2021 title. Remember when Kahleah Copper was supposed to be the new face of the franchise? Remember when it was Angel Reese?</p><p>The Sky traded up to draft Reese in 2024, touted her as the future, then flipped her for future first-round picks. Don’t fans have the right to protect themselves from further heartbreak?</p><p>At the request of media, Pagliocca addressed his offseason dealings at length Wednesday, including the blockbuster trade of Reese.</p><p><b>On how the Reese deal went down:</b> ‘‘It was a decision we made together. We remained in constant contact with Angel and her team after the season. Over that time, there were conversations with Atlanta. They expressed interest. That was a destination that was favorable for her.’’</p><p><b>On the return in the deal:</b> ‘‘If the return wasn’t what we were looking for, we weren’t going to move Angel. It had to be favorable for both sides. It’s not often you get two first-round picks back.’’</p><p><b>On whether Reese’s suspension last season was a factor:</b> ‘‘[The trade] was nothing that was contentious at any point. We had closed that chapter [on the suspension]. Just a competitive player. Things were said. She handled it. We moved forward.’’</p><p><b>On whether the ‘‘Twin Towers’’ pairing of Reese and Cardoso failed:</b> ‘‘There’s a lot of games where they played extremely well together. But systems change, people change, coaching staffs change. We have to make sure that we’re fitting players that are going to fit the best in Tyler’s system.’’</p><p><b>On what ‘‘roster balance’’ really meant: </b>‘‘Clearly, I’ve been aggressive [in getting] players that I think are going to fit the way that I want to play, that Tyler wants to play. That balance sometimes means we have to trade a player to make sure we can rebuild some future draft capital.’’</p><p>That’s about as candid an explanation as you’ll hear from a GM in public.</p><p>Are there more layers to the trade? You <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/chicago-sky/2026/04/12/two-teams-two-timelines-two-different-bets-on-angel-reese" target="_blank" ><u>bet</u></a>. Will the full story ever come to light? Probably not. On some level, fans will have to take another leap of faith.</p><p>And, at least for now, the energy at camp backs it up. Just listen to Jackson.</p><p>‘‘I mean, it’s only Day 3, and I honestly want to run through a wall for Tyler, for Jeff,’’ she said. ‘‘Like, I’ve truly felt welcome here. I truly feel seen from the beginning, and that’s just something that I’ve been missing in my career — just feeling wanted, just feeling like, ‘You see me as I see myself.’ ’’</p><p>Maybe the Jackson-Cardoso duo is here to stay. </p><bsp-list-loadmore class="PageListH" data-module data-hide-authors="true" data-hide-categories="true" data-hide-dates="true" data-show-loadmore="true" ><a class="AnchorLink" id="list-010001" name="list-010001"></a><div class="PageList-header" > <div class="PageList-header-title-wrap" > <div class="PageList-header-title"> Latest on the Sky and WNBA </div> </div> </div> <div class="PageListH-items" data-with-borders data-list-loadmore-items> <div class="PageListH-items-item"><div class="PagePromoB"> <div class="PagePromo-media"><a class="Link" aria-label="WNBA has record 216-game TV and streaming schedule across several outlets" entries.title: Inside-the-park homer feels right as career-first for White Sox rookie grinder Sam Antonacci
entries.summary: <p>PHOENIX — <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox" target="_blank" ><u>White Sox</u></a> utility player <b>Sam Antonacci</b> can hit the ball over the fence. He hit seven home runs in the minor leagues and one in the World Baseball Classic for <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/03/12/white-sox-prospect-sam-antonacci-turning-heads-at-wbc-hes-made-a-tremendous-impression" target="_blank" ><u>Team Italy</u></a>.</p><p>But it just feels right that Antonacci’s first major-league four-bagger was of the inside-the-park variety.</p><p>‘‘No one was putting their hands up, so I just kept running,’’ Antonacci, 23, said Tuesday after becoming the first Sox player to have to leg out his first career homer since <b>Kevin Bell</b> in 1976. ‘‘[It] kind of exemplifies who I am as a player. . . .</p><p>‘‘I feel like a normal player would just cruise in, standing up at second. [I] just wanted the extra base. I wasn’t going to say anything; I was just going to take it.’’</p><p>And take it he did after Diamondbacks left fielder <b>Lourdes Gurriel Jr. </b>took it for granted that the play was dead after a field attendant touched Antonacci’s grounder down the line. Umpires didn’t stop the play, however. And although replay clearly showed interference, they ruled it wasn’t reviewable.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">INSIDE-THE-PARK HOME RUN FOR SAM ANTONACCI 😮‍💨 <a href="https://t.co/cjgADPis9X">pic.twitter.com/cjgADPis9X</a></p>— Chicago White Sox (@whitesox) <a href="https://twitter.com/whitesox/status/2046808511703822591?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">April 22, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p>Antonacci didn’t sweat it out while the umps conferred on the controversial play, but he was gasping for air.</p><p>‘‘I was honestly catching my breath,’’ he said, laughing. ‘‘I was a little out of shape on that run.’’</p><p>He came closer to a garden-variety inaugural homer earlier in the game, crushing a 408-foot drive that caromed off the wall in right-center field. He settled for his first career triple on a three-RBI night that followed a tough <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/04/15/sam-antonacci-called-up-white-sox" target="_blank" ><u>first week</u></a> at the plate (2-for-16) since his call-up April 15.</p><p>‘‘A little bit of a slow start, but I like where I’ve been at and the at-bats I’ve been taking,’’ Antonacci said.</p><p>Infielder <b>Colson Montgomery</b>, who hit one of the Sox’ three other homers in <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/04/21/munetaka-murakami-homer-four-straight-games-white-sox-arizona" target="_blank" ><u>the series-opening victory</u></a>, said he gladly would’ve taken an inside-the-parker over the one he lined around the right-field foul pole for his first homer <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2025/07/22/colson-montgomery-first-homer-white-sox-streak" target="_blank" ><u>last July</u></a> in Tampa, Florida.</p><p>‘‘That’s sick,’’ he said. ‘‘That’s unique.’’</p><p>But color <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/04/22/mune-ball-murakami-white-sox-home-runs-offense-colson-montgomery" target="_blank" ><u>power-hitting first baseman</u></a> <b>Munetaka Murakami </b>unimpressed with Antonacci’s first dinger.</p><p>‘‘I want him to hit a normal home run,’’ he joked.</p><h3><b>Mune’s no slug</b></h3><p>Murakami hustled out a pair of infield singles Tuesday, the first on a 100 mph rocket that got stuck in third baseman <b>Nolan Arenado</b>’s glove and the second on a screaming grounder to shortstop <b>Geraldo Perdomo</b>, whose throw couldn’t beat him to first.</p><p>‘‘I think [manager] <b>Will</b> [<b>Venable</b>] thinks I’m a bit slow on the feet, so I’m just going to try to tell him that I can kind of run fast,’’ said Murakami, who’s pushing Venable for a green light to attempt his first career steal.</p><p>‘‘We saw his speed down the line today, which was cool; he runs hard,” Venable said. ‘‘He’s really filling out his game. It’s more than just the slug, as we’re seeing, and the slug is impressive, as well.’’</p><h3><b>Burke back on the board</b></h3><p>Right-hander <b>Sean Burke</b>’s six serviceable innings Tuesday earned him his first victory in 10 months. He had gone 16 appearances (11 starts) since his last one, which also was against the D-backs on June 25, 2025.</p><p>‘‘I didn’t know,’’ said Burke, who’s 1-2 with a 4.10 ERA in 26⅓ innings spanning five outings this season. ‘‘That’s too long. Can’t let that happen again.’’</p>
entries.author: Mitchell Armentrout

entries.title: Man killed, 3 women injured in Little Village shooting
entries.summary: <p>A man was killed and three women were injured in a shooting in the Little Village neighborhood Wednesday afternoon, police said.</p><p>It occurred around 4:30 p.m. as a man and three women stood near the sidewalk in the 3400 block of West 26th Street. An unknown shooter opened fire, hitting all four of them, police said. </p><p>The man, 46, was shot several times and transported to Mount Sinai Hospital where he was pronounced dead. His name has not been released.</p><p>Of the three women:<br></p><ul class="rte2-style-ul" id="rte-d9628172-3eaa-11f1-9977-bde3ac8f36ca"><li>One, age unknown, was shot in her lower back and made her way to Mount Sinai Hospital, where she was listed in critical condition.</li><li>The second, age 25, was shot in her face and was transported to Mount Sinai, where she was listed in fair condition.</li><li>The third, age 47, was shot in her arm and transported to Mount Sinai, where she was listed in fair condition. </li></ul><p>Area 4 detectives are investigating. <br></p>
entries.author: Sun-Times Wire

entries.title: Murakami homers for 5th straight game as Montgomery, Vargas keep up White Sox’ siege in loss
entries.summary: <p>PHOENIX —Make it five in a row for raking <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox" target="_blank" ><u>White Sox</u></a> rookie Munetaka Murakami, who homered in yet another game Wednesday as his teammates Colson Montgomery and Miguel Vargas continued their own stunning long-ball streaks against the Diamondbacks.</p><p>It still wasn’t enough as Arizona launched their own home run barrage against Sox starter Anthony Kay, who surrendered eight runs in an 11-7 loss.</p><p>But Murakami’s <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/04/22/mune-ball-murakami-white-sox-home-runs-offense-colson-montgomery" target="_blank" ><u>latest moonshot</u></a> was his longest yet, a 451-foot, two-run rainbow to left field in the seventh inning off D-backs reliever Ryan Thompson for his 10th of the season, passing Aaron Judge for the second-most in Major League Baseball.</p><p>The rookie's five-game homer streak has also tied the franchise record previously matched by Paul Konerko, A.J. Pierzynski, Carlos Lee, Frank Thomas, Ron Kittle and Greg Luzinski. </p><p>"I’m really happy that I have the record," Murakami said via interpreter Kenzo Yagi. "At any plate appearance, I stay the same. I’m very focused on each and every at-bat."</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">FIVE STRAIGHT FOR NO. 5️⃣ <a href="https://t.co/Ndy8cumnXW">pic.twitter.com/Ndy8cumnXW</a></p>— Chicago White Sox (@whitesox) <a href="https://twitter.com/whitesox/status/2047164790850666703?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">April 23, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p>Vargas’ third-inning blast off D-backs starter Eduardo Rodriguez was his third in as many games, sailing 428 feet with an exit velocity of 109 mph.</p><p>"It’s just baseball. Sometimes you are hitting the ball good and you don’t get any results. Sometimes you just get lucky," Vargas said. "Right now, I think we got a good spot. We have a really quality at-bats as a team. We trust each other."</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">THREE STRAIGHT GAMES with a home run for Miguel Vargas ‼️ <a href="https://t.co/Ju2tXFfCRk">pic.twitter.com/Ju2tXFfCRk</a></p>— Chicago White Sox (@whitesox) <a href="https://twitter.com/whitesox/status/2047141694856954211?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">April 23, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p>And there was no doubt about Montgomery’s fourth dinger in four games, either, at nearly 106 mph off the bat and 423 feet into the right-field stands at Chase Field.</p><p>"They just continue to fight. It's one guy after the other, putting good swings together," manager Will Venable said. </p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">"atta boy, Colson" - Sean Burke <a href="https://t.co/vGNNVoFYGJ">pic.twitter.com/vGNNVoFYGJ</a></p>— Chicago White Sox (@whitesox) <a href="https://twitter.com/whitesox/status/2047146407526572416?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">April 23, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p>The Sox have racked up 15 home runs in their last five games, with Vargas, Montgomery and Murakami accounting for 12 of them at the heart of the order.</p><p>"We’re definitely influencing each other and happy the three of us hit, and all of the lineup getting a lot of runs in is obviously a good thing," Murakami said. "Other than that, I’m really disappointed that we lost today. We’ve just got to get out there tomorrow and really win the series."</p>
entries.author: Mitchell Armentrout

entries.title: Horoscope for Thursday, April 23, 2026
entries.summary: <h2>Moon Alert</h2> <p>Avoid shopping (except food and gas) and important decisions from 2 p.m. to 3 p.m. After that, the moon moves from Cancer into Leo.</p><h2>Aries (March 21-April 19)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Keep your eyes open because it's possible you can get richer today. It might be in a tiny way, but it could be in a bigger way. (One can only hope.) The bottom line is that unexpected gifts, goodies and favors from others will come your way today. Keep your pockets open!</p><h2>Taurus (April 20-May 20)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> This is a favorable day because the sun, Venus and Uranus are all in Taurus, and your ruler Venus is lined up with unpredictable Uranus. Unexpected events and exciting new experiences might occur, especially through love and romance. Perhaps a new relationship with someone unusual?</p><h2>Gemini (May 21-June 20)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> This is probably a restless day for you. Even though you feel keen to talk to others, and you might also feel competitive, you're a bit unsure of your direction. Unusual money-making ideas might occur to you. Spontaneous ways to spend your money might also occur. (Keep your receipts.)</p><h2>Cancer (June 21-July 22)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> This is the classic day to meet someone unusual — perhaps a friend or an acquaintance in a group setting. This person could be a romantic interest, or not. Quite likely, they will introduce something unusual or unexpected into your world.</p><h2>Leo (July 23-Aug. 22)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Today you might encounter an unusual authority figure. ("Hi Darth.") Quite possibly, this conversation will make you feel restless, but it might also inspire you in some way. An unexpected advantage — even money — might be the result. Stay open to all possibilities.</p><h2>Virgo (Aug. 23-Sept. 22)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-7b0000" name="html-embed-module-7b0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>A <b>positive</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Unexpected opportunities to travel might fall in your lap today. If so, act quickly because this window of opportunity will be brief. Someone unusual might enter your world, introducing you to new ideas, new knowledge and a greater sense of freedom.</p><h2>Libra (Sept. 23-Oct. 22)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> You're high-viz today! People notice you more than usual. Quite possibly, something unexpected will occur that causes this. Certainly, this is the kind of day where you can benefit from the wealth and resources of someone else. Don't hesitate — say yes.</p><h2>Scorpio (Oct. 23-Nov. 21)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Don't be surprised if someone close to you makes an unusual suggestion today. They might have news about potential travel plans or something to do with higher education, medicine or the law. Whatever it is will likely please you. (You will have to make a fast decision.)</p><h2>Sagittarius (Nov. 22-Dec. 21)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Your work routine will be interrupted today by something unexpected, but it could be pleasant. New equipment, unexpected assistance, work-related travel or unusual people appearing in your world are just some examples. Stay on your toes!</p><h2>Capricorn (Dec. 22-Jan. 19)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> A new romance or an unexpected flirtation with someone who is "different" might occur today. Meanwhile, today holds all kinds of fun-loving opportunities. Accept invitations to socialize, especially if they involve learning something new or meeting new people. Live it up!</p><h2>Aquarius (Jan. 20-Feb. 18)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Stock the fridge because spontaneous get-togethers and surprise entertaining might occur for you. This could be related to your work or something to do with a pet. You might also buy something beautiful, artistic or high-tech for where you live. It’s an interesting day!</p><h2>Pisces (Feb. 19-March 20)</h2> <p></p> <div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="HtmlModule"><a class="AnchorLink" id="html-embed-module-4d0000" name="html-embed-module-4d0000"></a><div class="HoroscopeStarbox HoroscopeStarbox_five"> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_container"> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> <svg class="HoroscopeStar"><use xlink:href="#icon-star"></use></svg> </div> <div class="HoroscopeStarbox_description"> <p>An <b>average</b> day</p> </div> </div> <style> .HoroscopeStarbox { background: #f8f8f8; display: flex; align-items: center; } .HoroscopeStarbox_container { display: grid; grid-gap: 5px; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr 1fr; padding: 20px; width: 40%; min-width: 160px; max-width: 200px; } .HoroscopeStarbox_description { padding: 20px; font-family: var(--font-1); font-size: var(--body-2); } .HoroscopeStarbox_container svg { object-fit: cover; aspect-ratio: 1 / 1; width: 100%; } </style> </div> </div><p> Keep your wits about you today because this is a mildly accident-prone day. However, it's an exciting day because you will see new places, meet new faces and have thrilling new ideas. New romance or fun, unexpected outings will delight. Parents should keep an eye on their children to avoid accidents.</p><h2>If today is your birthday</h2><p>Actor and wrestler John Cena (1977) shares your birthday today. You're compassionate, intellectual and inquisitive. You enjoy talking to people and have a strong sense of justice. This is a year of new beginnings, adventures and major changes. Keep your eyes open for new opportunities. Take action and initiative and define your goals. Prepare for leadership.</p>
entries.author: Georgia Nicols

entries.title: Cubs win eighth straight behind Matthew Boyd's return, Ben Brown's relief work
entries.summary: <p><b>Matthew Boyd </b>finally joined the party. <b>Michael Busch</b> left the premises for the first time this season. <b>Ben Brown</b>, the most reliable two-inning pitcher in a <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/cubs" >Cubs</a> uniform, tacked a “W” next to his name for the first time in almost a year. And if you’re looking for somebody worth investing in on the prediction market, locker mates <b>Alex Bregman</b> and <b>Pete Crow-Armstrong</b> might be just the guys.</p><p>The Cubs (15-9) won their eighth straight game Wednesday night, beating the Phillies 7-2 before a crowd of 29,951. “Go, Cubs, Go” is an earworm.</p><p>The Cubs had not won eight straight in April since the 1970 Cubs won 11 straight, 56 years ago. That team finished second place in an era where being runner-up didn’t buy you a wild-card invite. The lesson there? Enjoy how well your team is playing, but no need to set aside money for the playoffs just yet.</p><p>The Phillies (8-16), who have been October participants in each of the last five seasons, have lost eight straight, including their last four meetings to the Cubs by a combined score of 40-13.</p><p>They might one day view <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/cubs/2026/04/22/wrigley-rat-spooks-phillies-amuses-cubs-fans" >the rat who trod on their toes Tuesday</a> with the same superstitious terror that fans of the ’69 Cubs had of the black cat that stalked <b>Ron Santo</b> near the Shea Stadium dugout.</p><p>Don’t believe in omens? Try telling that to the Phillies on a night that a ball originally ruled a home run, by <b>Aroldis Garcia</b>, was declared foul, center fielder <b>Justin Crawford</b> dropped a fly ball that acted possessed in the wind and the game ended when ABS changed <b>Hoby Milner’s</b> final pitch of the night to strike three on <b>Rafael Marchan</b>.</p><p>Boyd, who came off the 15-day injured list Wednesday, allowed two runs in 4‰ innings, ending his night by inducing <b>Kyle Schwarber</b> to hit into a double play when the Phillies were still in striking distance.</p><p>Busch hit his first home run in his 83rd at-bat of the season, a third-inning solo shot that was followed two innings later by <b>Seiya Suzuki’s</b> two-run homer.</p><p>Brown relieved Boyd and was credited with the win. And Bregman and Crow-Armstrong each collected three hits apiece.</p><p>“Grateful to be back, grateful to be competing,’’ Boyd said on a night four Cubs pitchers did not walk a batter while striking out 12. “[The streak] is awesome. It just speaks to the professional approach we have in this clubhouse — the experience of the guys we have in this clubhouse.’’</p><h3>Traffic jam</h3><p>If you thought the Cubs had an unusual amount of traffic on the bases Tuesday night, you were not wrong. While beating the Phillies 7-4, they left 17 men on base. That matched the team record for a nine-inning game set July 3, 2010, and came within one of the National League record shared by multiple teams.</p><p>The Yankees hold the big-league record of 20 set in a 13-7 loss to the Red Sox on Sept. 21, 1956.</p><p>The White Sox are one of three teams to leave 19 men on base, back on April 7, 1977, when they lost 9-5 to Toronto in the first game in Blue Jays history — in unsuitable playing conditions.</p><bsp-listcarousel class="PageListE" data-module data-column-count=3 data-hide-authors="true" data-hide-categories="true" data-hide-dates="true" entries.author: Gordon Edes

entries.title: 'Mune-ball': White Sox’ offense firing on all cylinders with Murakami at the wheel
entries.summary: <p>PHOENIX — Welcome to the ‘‘Mune-ball’’ era for the <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox" target="_blank" ><u>White Sox</u></a>.</p><p>They’re scrimping with an $82 million payroll, the third-lowest in Major League Baseball. They’re running out a slew of rookies and reclamation projects — some of them learning new positions — to get their bats in the lineup. And they’re hitting the snot out of the ball, at least in the last week.</p><p>While general manager Chris Getz’s rebuilding team might not be on a trajectory for a data-driven, Hollywood-written playoff run, a la Billy Beane’s ‘‘Moneyball’’-era Athletics, Getz’s first splashy free-agent splurge has super-charged an offense that finally is making Sox games fun to watch again.</p><p>‘‘We’re very much connecting from top to bottom in the lineup, and I think it’s just really important that we really continue as a team to get good results,’’ Japanese slugger Munetaka Murakami said through an interpreter after hitting a home run <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/04/21/munetaka-murakami-homer-four-straight-games-white-sox-arizona" target="_blank" ><u>for the fourth consecutive game</u></a> of the Sox’ West Coast trip Tuesday against the Diamondbacks.</p><p>Murakami’s two-year, $34 million contract has looked like a bigger bargain with every foot of his blasts. Entering play Wednesday, his last four homers collectively had traveled a third of a mile. And that was before he made it five games in a row with a homer with a two-run blast in the seventh inning. His 10 homers are one behind MLB leader Yordan Alvarez.</p><p>‘‘I’m really analyzing the pitchers as much as possible so that I’m getting ready into the at-bats,’’ said Murakami, who regularly admires his towering shots out of the box but is quick to share credit with his teammates. ‘‘So I’ll just try to keep doing whatever I’m doing right now.’’</p><p>Colson Montgomery, <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/03/31/white-sox-miguel-vargas-miami-grand-slam" target="_blank" >Miguel Vargas</a> and other offensive cogs also would do well to keep doing what they’ve done against the A’s and Diamondbacks. Murakami, Vargas and Montgomery launched the Sox’ first back-to-back-to-back blasts in six years Tuesday. Vargas and Montgomery homered again Wednesday, <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/04/22/munetaka-murakami-home-run-fifth-straight-colson-montgomery-miguel-vargas-white-sox" >as well</a>.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center><div class="Enhancement-item"> <div class="ExternalContent-wrapper" > <div class="TweetUrl"> <blockquote class="twitter-tweet"><p lang="en" dir="ltr">on repeat 🔁 <a href="https://t.co/y1zELxyS5e">pic.twitter.com/y1zELxyS5e</a></p>— Chicago White Sox (@whitesox) <a href="https://twitter.com/whitesox/status/2046990514365296906?ref_src=twsrc%5Etfw">April 22, 2026</a></blockquote> <script async src="https://platform.twitter.com/widgets.js" charset="utf-8"></script> </div> </div> </div></div><p>It all has helped the Sox jump from MLB’s worst team OPS entering their trip to 17th (.702) and shake off mid-April vibes that <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/white-sox/2026/04/16/white-sox-head-west-with-that-familiar-losing-feeling-rays" target="_blank" ><u>scarcely could have been worse</u></a> after getting swept at home by the Rays. Their .985 OPS and 17 homers over their last seven games are both tops in the majors.</p><p>Murakami’s the one getting the international spotlight, but ‘‘I think it’s just anyone,’’ Montgomery said. ‘‘Everything’s contagious. Hits are contagious. Good energy is contagious. And I think that’s what we’re doing right now.</p><p>‘‘It’s just settling in. A lot of us, we’re younger guys and we haven’t really played in April, kicked off the season, stuff like that. Everyone is playing free, easy and just committed to their plan.’’</p><p>After the batting order hit a drought during the first couple weeks of the season, manager Will Venable said it is enjoying a ‘‘good regression to that mean, where we’re able to get actual results.’’</p><p>‘‘You’ve gotta slug in this league,’’ Venable said. ‘‘No matter when it is in the count, you’ve got to take your shot. I think we do have a number of guys [who] can leave the ballpark and slug. And so those are the guys that you’ve got to have take some shots.</p><p>‘‘We have a number of guys that maybe that’s not part of their game, and those are the guys that really need to be grinding at-bats, having that Plan B swing, shooting the ball the other way and really extending the starting pitcher and working at-bats.</p><p>‘‘I think that’s a good thing about our offense: We have a bunch of different guys that can make an impact in different ways. We’ve just got to string it all together.’’</p><p>The Sox’ power surge has left only two questions: How long can they keep it up? And when will they come up with a regular home-run celebration in the dugout?</p><p>‘‘We’re working on it right now; we have something in mind that’s coming,’’ Montgomery teased. ‘‘We don’t really want to tell anyone yet, but you’ll notice it.’’</p><div class="PageListL" data-module data-hide-authors="true" data-hide-categories="true" data-hide-dates="true" data-show-loadmore="true" ><a class="AnchorLink" id="list-4a0005" name="list-4a0005"></a><div class="PageList-header" > <div class="PageList-header-title-wrap" > <div class="PageList-header-title"> <a class=""
